wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the synaptix-studio-website-app project. Explain its purpose as a marketing and lead-generation platform for an AI automation agency, highlighting core features such as AI tools, blog content management, and interactive lead capture. Describe the high-level architecture as a React-based single-page application built with Vite and TypeScript. Document the relationship between major components like the AI tools suite, admin dashboard, and user-facing pages. Include implementation details such as hash-based routing, component composition, and integration with Supabase and Google GenAI. Provide a beginner-friendly overview of how the application functions from user entry to conversion, and explain how developers can extend or customize the system. Use terminology consistent with the codebase, including references to AIToolsPage, BlogAdminDashboard, and ContactSection. Include practical examples of user workflows and system interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the core layout components of the application. Explain the role of App.tsx in orchestrating routing and global state, and how it composes major UI sections. Detail the implementation of Header and Footer components, including navigation handling, theme toggling, and responsive behavior using Tailwind CSS. Describe the HeroSection’s integration with interactive elements like the ViralityMeter and dynamic animations. Document the use of Modal and Background components for overlay experiences and visual layering. Include examples of component composition, accessibility practices, and responsive breakpoints. Address common issues such as z-index conflicts and hydration mismatches, and provide best practices for extending layout components while maintaining consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Supabase integration implemented in supabase.ts. Detail the configuration setup, authentication flow, and CRUD operations for blog posts and user-generated content. Document the public methods exposed by the service, including their parameters, return types, and error handling mechanisms. Explain how the service manages database access via Supabase client, including real-time subscriptions and row-level security policies. Provide concrete examples from ContactSection.tsx and BlogPage.tsx showing form data submission and content retrieval. Include guidance on handling session management, API key security via environment variables, and data validation before insertion. Address common issues such as connection timeouts, permission denied errors, and offline behavior. Discuss performance optimization strategies like query filtering, indexing, and caching with React Query patterns if applicable. Document how to extend the service for new data models while maintaining type safety with TypeScript interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Ad Copy Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Ad Copy Generator tool. Thoroughly explain its implementation using React components and Google GenAI integration to generate compelling ad copy based on user inputs such as product description, target audience, and tone. Document the component's structure, including form inputs, state management via React hooks, and the API call flow to Google GenAI. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how user input is processed and formatted before being sent to the AI model. Explain the response handling, result rendering, and copy-to-clipboard functionality. Address common issues such as empty responses or API rate limits, and provide troubleshooting guidance. Discuss performance optimization techniques like input validation and loading states to enhance user experience. Make the content accessible to beginners while offering technical depth for advanced developers looking to customize or extend the tool.

wiki_catalogs.name: Public Blog Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the public-facing blog features of the Synaptix Studio website. Explain how BlogPage fetches and displays a list of published articles using data from Supabase, including filtering, sorting, and pagination logic if applicable. Describe the role of ArticlePage in rendering individual blog posts with proper metadata (title, date, author) and content structure. Detail the implementation of BlogMarkdownRenderer for parsing and securely rendering Markdown content with syntax highlighting and embedded media support. Document the routing strategy using hash-based navigation for blog URLs and SEO best practices implemented, such as meta tags and structured data. Include examples of responsive design patterns for article layouts and performance optimizations like lazy loading of images. Address common issues such as broken links in Markdown, malformed content rendering, and caching strategies for faster load times.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Ad Copy Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Ad Copy Generator component. Explain its purpose as a tool that generates high-converting ad variations using Google GenAI, tailored to specific platforms like Facebook, Google Ads, and LinkedIn. Document the implementation details including form handling with state management via React hooks (useState), input validation, and submission logic that constructs a structured prompt for the Gemini API. Highlight the use of system instructions and response schema to enforce JSON output with ad variations containing headline, body, image suggestion, and CTA. Describe the UI/UX flow from user input to result rendering via AdCard components, including loading states with DynamicLoader and scroll-to-result behavior using refs and useEffect. Detail integration with analytics via trackEvent('generate_ad_copy') for usage monitoring. Provide a concrete example showing how changing the ad platform affects the generated copy style. Address common issues such as API errors, empty inputs, and rate limiting, and include performance optimization tips like lazy loading the results section. Explain how to extend this component with new ad platforms or additional output fields.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the technology stack of synaptix-studio-website-app. Detail the core frameworks and libraries including React for UI rendering, Vite as the build tool, TypeScript for type safety, and Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling. Explain the role of each dependency listed in package.json, particularly critical ones like @supabase/supabase-js for database operations, google-generative-ai for AI content generation, and vitest for testing. Document configuration files such as vite.config.ts (module resolution, plugins), tsconfig.json (type checking settings), and vercel.json (deployment configuration). Include practical examples of how these technologies integrate, such as Vite's development server setup in dev-server.js or environment variable handling. Address version compatibility, security considerations, and performance implications of the chosen stack. Provide guidance for developers on extending or replacing components of the stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Tools Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI tools component suite. Explain how AIToolsPage serves as a container for individual AI tool sections, each encapsulating a specific functionality like ad copy generation or email subject testing. Document the shared patterns across these components, including form handling, API integration with Google GenAI via service layers, and real-time result rendering. Highlight the use of state management with React hooks to manage user inputs and response states. Provide concrete examples from AIAdCopyGeneratorSection and AIEmailSubjectLineTesterSection to illustrate input validation, loading states, and error handling. Address integration with analytics services for tracking tool usage. Include guidance on adding new AI tools following existing architectural patterns and ensuring UI consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Google Analytics integration handled by analytics.ts. Explain the initialization process of the analytics tracker, event naming conventions, and the tracking wrapper functions implemented in the service. Document all tracked user interactions including page views, button clicks, tool usage events, and form submissions with examples from HeroSection and AIToolsPage. Detail how events are structured with categories, actions, and labels following best practices. Include implementation patterns for conditional tracking based on user consent via CookieConsentBanner. Address error handling for failed pings, debouncing mechanisms, and GDPR compliance considerations. Provide guidance on adding new custom events, ensuring consistency in telemetry data, and avoiding over-tracking. Discuss integration with Google Tag Manager if applicable and how to validate event firing using browser developer tools. Include performance impact analysis and lazy-loading strategies for the analytics script.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Email Subject Line Tester
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Email Subject Line Tester tool. Explain its implementation using React and Google GenAI to analyze and score email subject lines for engagement potential. Document the component's UI structure, including input fields, scoring logic, and feedback display. Detail how user-submitted subject lines are processed and sent to Google GenAI with specific prompts to generate scores and improvement suggestions. Include examples from the actual code showing state management, API request formatting, and response parsing. Describe the visual feedback mechanism, such as score indicators and revision recommendations. Address common issues like low-scoring results or ambiguous feedback, and provide best practices for crafting effective subject lines. Cover performance considerations including response latency and UI responsiveness during AI processing. Ensure content is approachable for new users while providing technical insights for developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the admin-side blog management tools. Explain how BlogAdminDashboard provides an interface for authenticated users to view, create, edit, and delete blog posts. Document the authentication flow using PasswordModal to secure access to the admin panel, including session management and protection against unauthorized access. Describe the functionality of BlogEditorModal, including rich text or Markdown input, preview capabilities, metadata fields (title, slug, publish date, featured image), and save/publish workflows. Detail how the supabase.ts service handles CRUD operations for blog posts, including data validation, error handling, and synchronization between the admin interface and the frontend. Include information on draft vs. published states, content moderation if applicable, and integration with the public BlogPage and ArticlePage components. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common issues like failed saves, authentication timeouts, and data consistency errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Email Subject Line Tester
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Email Subject Line Tester component. Explain its function in analyzing and scoring email subject lines for open rate potential using Google GenAI. Document the component's structure, including form controls for subject line, audience context, and topic context, managed through React state hooks. Detail the handleSubmit function that sends a prompt with optional contextual information to the Gemini API with a strict JSON response schema defining score, analysis (clarity, urgency, curiosity, spamRisk), and three improved suggestions. Describe the result rendering flow, including the ViralityMeter component for visualizing the score and SuggestionItem components for alternative subject lines. Highlight the use of loading states, error handling, and smooth scrolling to results via refs. Include integration with analytics via trackEvent('test_subject_line'). Provide a practical example demonstrating how adding audience context improves suggestion relevance. Address common pitfalls like malformed API responses or missing environment variables, and offer troubleshooting guidance. Explain customization options such as adjusting the scoring criteria or extending the analysis dimensions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the component-based architecture of the application. Explain the design philosophy of breaking the UI into reusable, self-contained React components organized under the /components directory. Describe the role of App.tsx as the root component managing routing and global state. Document the structure and responsibilities of key layout components such as Header, Footer, and HeroSection, including their props interfaces and state management patterns. Illustrate component composition patterns used throughout the codebase, such as how AIToolsPage composes multiple AI tool sections. Include examples of functional components using React hooks for state and side effects. Address accessibility considerations, responsive design implementation via Tailwind CSS, and component lifecycle management. Provide best practices for creating new components that align with existing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin component suite used for content and configuration management. Explain the purpose of BlogAdminDashboard as the central interface for managing blog posts, including CRUD operations and integration with Supabase. Describe the role of modal components like BlogEditorModal and PasswordModal in providing secure, isolated editing environments. Document the use of form state, validation, and submission flows within these components. Illustrate how AiLinkManagerModal enables management of AI-generated resource links. Address security considerations such as password protection and access control. Provide examples of how these components interact with the supabase.ts service for data persistence. Include best practices for extending admin functionality while maintaining security and usability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Third-Party API Connections
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for third-party API integrations including Brave Search API via brave.js and Google GenAI used across AI tools. For brave.js, document the HTTP client implementation, search query construction, response parsing, and error handling for network failures or rate limits. Explain how the API key is securely injected via environment variables and abstracted through the service layer. For AI tools, detail how Google GenAI endpoints are invoked from components like AIAdCopyGeneratorSection to generate content, including prompt engineering patterns, request payload structure, and streaming responses. Document the retry logic, timeout configurations, and fallback mechanisms implemented in these services. Address security practices such as input sanitization, output validation, and protection against injection attacks. Include performance considerations like request batching, caching strategies in iconCache.ts, and preloading assets via iconPreload.ts. Provide step-by-step guidance for integrating additional third-party APIs following the existing architectural patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Idea Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Idea Generator tool. Explain its implementation using React and Google GenAI to generate creative business, marketing, or content ideas based on user-defined categories and constraints. Document the component's architecture, including form controls, dynamic prompt construction, and asynchronous AI response handling. Provide code examples showing how user inputs are transformed into structured prompts for Google GenAI and how generated ideas are rendered in a user-friendly format. Describe features like idea categorization, copy functionality, and potential integration with other tools. Address common issues such as repetitive outputs or irrelevant suggestions, and offer strategies to refine prompts for better results. Discuss performance aspects including loading states and error handling during API calls. Balance beginner-friendly explanations with technical details for customization.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Idea Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Idea Generator component. Explain its role in helping users brainstorm business ideas based on industry, business size, primary goal, and specific challenges. Document the implementation using React functional components with state management for form inputs and results. Detail the handleSubmit logic that constructs a contextual prompt for Google GenAI, requesting a list of innovative ideas with descriptions and implementation steps. Highlight the use of navigate prop for internal routing when users click CTAs, enabling seamless navigation to the AI strategy section. Describe the UI layout with form controls, loading states, and idea cards for displaying results. Emphasize the integration with analytics to track idea generation events. Provide a code example showing how user inputs are combined into a prompt and how the response is parsed and rendered. Address error handling for API failures and input validation. Discuss performance considerations such as debouncing inputs or caching frequent queries. Explain how to extend the component with new industries or goals by updating the static lists.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the routing and navigation system in synaptix-studio-website-app. Explain how client-side routing is implemented using a hash-based approach within App.tsx to enable SPA behavior without server-side routing. Document the navigation structure including main menu items in Header.tsx and specialized tool navigation in ToolsNavBar.tsx. Describe how route changes trigger component rendering and state updates. Include examples of navigation patterns, such as scrolling to sections or opening modals instead of full page changes. Address SEO implications of hash routing and how it interacts with the sitemap.xml and robots.txt files. Provide implementation details on how navigation events are handled and how active states are managed. Discuss potential enhancements like migrating to BrowserRouter or implementing dynamic route loading.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility and UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for utility and reusable UI components. Explain the performance optimization role of DynamicLoader and SkeletonLoader in lazy-loading content and managing perceived loading times. Describe Preloader’s function in asset prefetching and initial loading experience. Document the MarkdownRenderer and BlogMarkdownRenderer components for parsing and securely rendering markdown content, including syntax highlighting and link handling. Detail StyledText and Icon for consistent typography and iconography across the app. Explain ToolsNavBar’s role in navigation within the AI tools section. Include implementation details such as use of React.lazy, Suspense, and custom hooks like useOnScreen. Provide examples of usage, customization options, and performance impact. Address accessibility and SSR compatibility where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Website Auditor
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Website Auditor tool. Explain its implementation that allows users to input a website URL and receive an AI-generated analysis of design, content, and UX strengths and weaknesses. Document how the component captures URL input, validates it, and sends it as context to Google GenAI with a structured audit prompt. Include examples from the codebase showing the request-response cycle, result formatting, and presentation of audit findings in a readable report format. Describe the UI patterns used for displaying recommendations and potential improvements. Address common issues such as invalid URLs, timeout errors, or superficial analysis, and provide troubleshooting steps. Discuss performance considerations given the complexity of website analysis and potential latency. Ensure content is clear for non-technical users while offering implementation insights for developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Website Auditor
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Website Auditor component. Explain its functionality in analyzing a user-provided website URL and generating a comprehensive audit report using AI insights. Document the component's structure, including input form with URL validation, loading states, and result display sections. Detail the integration with Google GenAI to interpret website content and evaluate key aspects such as user experience, SEO, conversion optimization, and technical performance. Describe the expected JSON response schema from the AI model, including categories like 'Issues Found', 'Strengths', and 'Recommendations'. Highlight the use of state management to store and render audit results, and the implementation of smooth scrolling to results using refs. Include analytics tracking for audit generation events. Provide a practical example of how the system instruction guides the AI to perform a structured analysis. Address common issues such as invalid URLs, CORS restrictions during scraping (if applicable), and handling incomplete AI responses. Offer guidance on extending the audit categories or integrating with actual website scraping services for deeper analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS. Explain the utility-first approach employed throughout the codebase and how it enables rapid UI development directly in JSX. Document the configuration in tailwind.config.js (not visible but inferred), including theme extensions, color schemes for dark/light mode, and custom breakpoints. Describe how global styles are applied in App.tsx and index.html, and how component-specific styling is implemented using className attributes. Include examples of responsive design patterns using Tailwind's breakpoint prefixes and dark mode support via class toggling. Address accessibility considerations in color contrast and focus states. Provide guidance on creating consistent UI patterns, extending the design system, and optimizing for performance by minimizing unused CSS. Discuss how styling integrates with component logic and state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Knowledge Base Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Knowledge Base Generator tool. Explain its implementation that enables users to create structured knowledge base articles from raw information inputs. Document how the component uses React state to manage multi-field input and constructs a comprehensive prompt for Google GenAI to generate well-organized FAQ-style content. Include code examples showing the data flow from form submission to AI processing and the rendering of categorized Q&A pairs. Describe features like content formatting, copy-to-clipboard, and potential export options. Address common issues such as incomplete outputs or formatting inconsistencies, and provide guidance on optimizing input quality for better results. Discuss performance aspects including handling large text inputs and managing API response times. Make the documentation accessible to content creators while providing technical value for developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Knowledge Base Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Knowledge Base Generator component. Explain its purpose in helping users create structured knowledge base articles from a product or service description. Document the implementation using React hooks for managing user input (product name, description, target audience) and API response states. Detail the handleSubmit function that sends a prompt to Google GenAI requesting a well-organized knowledge base with sections like Overview, Key Features, Setup Guide, and Troubleshooting. Highlight the use of a defined response schema to ensure consistent JSON output with nested content structure. Describe the rendering of results into collapsible sections or markdown format for easy copying. Include integration with analytics to track usage and the use of navigate prop for contextual navigation. Provide a concrete example showing how a technical product description is transformed into user-friendly documentation. Address error handling for malformed responses and performance considerations for large outputs. Explain how to customize the template structure or add export functionality (e.g., PDF, Markdown).

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on state management patterns in the application. Explain how React's built-in useState and useEffect hooks are used for local component state, while App.tsx manages global state like theme and navigation. Detail the custom hooks in the /hooks directory, particularly useOnScreen for intersection detection and useLazyLoad for performance optimization. Document how state flows between components, such as form data in ContactSection being processed and submitted. Include examples of state persistence patterns, if any, and how state changes trigger re-renders. Address best practices for avoiding unnecessary re-renders and managing complex state logic. Discuss potential scalability issues and when to consider introducing a global state management library. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common state-related bugs.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Quiz Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Quiz Generator tool. Explain its implementation using React and Google GenAI to create interactive quizzes based on user-provided topics or content. Document the component's structure, including topic input, difficulty selection, and the process of generating quiz questions and multiple-choice answers. Provide examples from the codebase showing how prompts are constructed for Google GenAI, how responses are parsed into structured quiz data, and how the quiz is rendered interactively. Describe features like answer validation, scoring, and result display. Address common issues such as incorrect answer keys or ambiguous questions, and suggest ways to improve quiz quality through prompt engineering. Cover performance considerations including state management during quiz taking and loading times for AI generation. Ensure the documentation serves both end-users and developers effectively.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Quiz
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Quiz component. Explain its role as an interactive assessment tool that evaluates a user's AI readiness or marketing maturity. Document the component's state management for tracking current question index, user responses, and final score calculation. Detail the structure of quiz data (questions, options, scoring logic) and how it's rendered dynamically. Explain the submission flow where user answers are processed to generate a personalized result or recommendation. Highlight integration with analytics to track quiz completions and response patterns. Describe the UI elements such as progress indicators, answer selection feedback, and result visualization. Provide a practical example of how the quiz adapts based on user input and delivers actionable insights. Address accessibility considerations like keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Discuss extensibility options such as adding new quiz types, integrating with CRM systems, or generating shareable results. Include guidance on maintaining quiz content and ensuring logical scoring algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the API integration architecture. Document the service layer pattern implemented in the /services directory, with dedicated modules for Supabase, Google Analytics, and icon management. Explain how supabase.ts encapsulates database operations for blog content and user data, including authentication and CRUD operations. Detail how analytics.ts handles event tracking and integrates with Google Analytics. Describe the brave.js API integration for search functionality. Include examples of how components like ContactSection interact with these services to submit form data or retrieve content. Address error handling, retry mechanisms, and loading states. Discuss security considerations such as API key management and data validation. Provide guidelines for adding new API integrations and handling rate limiting.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Launchpad
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Launchpad component. Explain its function as a gateway or dashboard that introduces users to the suite of available AI tools. Document its minimalistic implementation as a navigational hub that may render tool previews, quick access buttons, or guided onboarding flows. Detail how it integrates with the main AIToolsPage via routing and hash navigation to direct users to specific tools. Highlight any state or context sharing mechanisms that preserve user progress across tools. Describe the UI design principles applied, such as visual hierarchy and consistent branding. Provide examples of how the launchpad enhances user experience by reducing cognitive load and improving tool discoverability. Address customization options for curating tool displays based on user segments or business goals. Explain how analytics track user engagement with the launchpad and individual tool entries. Discuss potential extensions like personalized recommendations or progress tracking for tool usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Tools Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the suite of AI-powered tools provided by Synaptix Studio. Explain the purpose of each tool including Ad Copy Generator, Email Subject Line Tester, Idea Generator, Website Auditor, Knowledge Base Generator, and Quiz Generator. Document the implementation details of how these tools use Google GenAI to process user inputs and generate responses. Describe the UI/UX patterns used across these tools, such as input forms, result displays, and copy-to-clipboard functionality. Include examples of the data flow from user interaction to AI processing and back to the interface. Address common use cases for each tool and provide best practices for achieving optimal results. Discuss performance considerations given the AI processing requirements and potential latency issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blog Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the blog content management system. Explain how the blog functionality allows Synaptix Studio to publish articles and manage content through an admin interface. Document the architecture of BlogPage for listing articles and ArticlePage for rendering individual posts using MarkdownRenderer. Describe the admin capabilities in BlogAdminDashboard, including authentication via PasswordModal and content editing through BlogEditorModal. Explain how blog data is stored in Supabase and retrieved via the supabase.ts service. Include details on the Markdown parsing implementation and any content moderation workflows. Address SEO considerations, URL routing for blog posts, and performance optimization for content delivery. Provide guidance for authors on formatting and publishing articles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the administrative interface for content management. Explain how the admin dashboard provides tools for managing blog content, AI tool links, and data tables. Document the authentication mechanism using PasswordModal to protect admin functionality. Describe the features of BlogEditorModal for creating and editing blog posts, and TableEditorModal for managing structured data. Include details on the UI design patterns used in the admin components, such as form validation and real-time previews. Explain how these admin components interact with the Supabase backend through the services layer. Provide step-by-step guidance for common administrative tasks and address security considerations for protecting sensitive functionality. Discuss potential extensions to the admin system for managing additional content types.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interaction Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on components that facilitate user engagement and conversion. Explain the purpose and implementation of interactive elements such as CalendlyModal for scheduling consultations, CookieConsentBanner for GDPR compliance, and ChatWidget for real-time support. Document how these components enhance user experience and drive business objectives like lead generation and customer support. Include details on their integration with third-party services (Calendly, chat platforms) and how they handle user data responsibly. Describe the visual design and behavior patterns, including animations and responsive interactions. Address accessibility requirements and performance impact of embedding external widgets. Provide configuration options and customization guidelines for adapting these components to different use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the testing approach for synaptix-studio-website-app. Explain how Vitest is used as the testing framework with a focus on unit testing critical components like ContactSection, Header, and HeroSection. Document the test setup in vitest.config.ts and how tests are organized under the src\test directory. Describe the testing patterns employed, such as mocking API calls, testing user interactions, and verifying component rendering. Include examples of test cases that validate form submissions, UI state changes, and integration with services. Address code coverage goals and how testing contributes to development workflow. Discuss limitations of the current testing approach and potential enhancements like adding end-to-end testing with Cypress or Playwright. Provide guidance for developers on writing effective tests for new components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build & Deployment Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the build process and deployment configuration. Explain how Vite is configured in vite.config.ts for development and production builds, including asset handling, code splitting, and environment variables. Document the deployment setup on Vercel through vercel.json, specifying routes, redirects, and edge function configurations. Describe the development workflow using dev-server.js and npm scripts defined in package.json. Include details on optimization techniques like code minification, asset compression, and preloading strategies implemented through iconPreload.ts. Address environment management, build caching, and deployment triggers. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common build failures and deployment issues. Discuss performance monitoring and how to verify successful deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on performance optimization techniques implemented in the application. Explain how lazy loading is achieved through DynamicLoader and useLazyLoad to defer loading of non-critical components. Document the preloading strategies using Preloader and skeleton screens with SkeletonLoader to improve perceived performance. Describe how these optimizations reduce initial load time and improve user experience, particularly on slower connections. Include metrics on performance improvements and how they're measured. Address the trade-offs between bundle size and functionality, and how code splitting is implemented. Provide guidelines for identifying performance bottlenecks and implementing additional optimizations. Discuss monitoring tools and techniques for maintaining optimal performance over time.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Comprehensive Project Analysis: Synaptix Studio Website App

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Synaptix Studio is an AI automation agency that helps businesses reduce costs and drive revenue through custom AI solutions. The website serves as a comprehensive platform to showcase their AI automation services, provide free AI tools, and generate leads through strategic content and interactive features.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Transform businesses with AI automation solutions that reduce costs and drive revenue
- Provide expert AI development, chatbots, and smart business systems
- Generate leads through interactive AI tools and strategy consultations
- Establish thought leadership in AI automation through content marketing
- Offer a seamless user experience with modern web technologies and responsive design

### Target Audience
- Startup founders looking to build MVPs with AI
- Established businesses seeking operational efficiency
- Solopreneurs needing automated business systems
- Web3 innovators requiring community management solutions
- Marketing agencies and consultants wanting to deliver more value
- E-commerce businesses aiming to automate customer interactions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows a modern React-based architecture with a component-driven design. The core components include:

```mermaid
graph TD
    App[App Component] --> Header[Header]
    App --> Router[Hash-based Router]
    App --> Footer[Footer]
    App --> ChatWidget[ChatWidget]
    App --> CookieConsent[CookieConsentBanner]
    App --> CalendlyModal[CalendlyModal]
    App --> PasswordModal[PasswordModal]
    App --> BlogAdminDashboard[BlogAdminDashboard]
    App --> MainContent[MainContent]
    MainContent --> HeroSection[HeroSection]
    MainContent --> ServicesSection[ServicesSection]
    MainContent --> HowItWorksSection[HowItWorksSection]
    MainContent --> ContactSection[ContactSection]
    MainContent --> PricingSection[PricingSection]
    MainContent --> AILaunchpadSection[AILaunchpadSection]
    MainContent --> ResourceCenterSection[ResourceCenterSection]
    MainContent --> TestimonialsSection[TestimonialsSection]
    MainContent --> FAQSection[FAQSection]
    MainContent --> LetsTalkSection[LetsTalkSection]
```

### Design Patterns
The application implements several modern design patterns:

- **Component Composition**: Complex UIs are built by composing smaller, reusable components
- **State Management**: Uses React's useState and useCallback hooks for efficient state management
- **Routing**: Implements a hash-based routing system for navigation without server-side routing
- **Lazy Loading**: Uses dynamic imports and loading states for better performance
- **Theme Management**: Implements a dark/light theme toggle with localStorage persistence
- **Form Handling**: Centralized form submission with error handling and analytics tracking

### System Relationships
The application connects to various external services and APIs:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client] --> B[API Layer]
    B --> C[Supabase Database]
    C --> D[(PostgreSQL)]
    A --> E[Google Analytics]
    A --> F[Calendly Widget]
    A --> G[Google Gemini AI]
    A --> H[Tailwind CSS CDN]
    A --> I[Partner Logos CDN]
```

### Data Flow
The data flows through the application in a structured manner:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interaction] --> B[Component State]
    B --> C[Form Submission]
    C --> D[Service Layer]
    D --> E[Supabase API]
    E --> F[Database]
    D --> G[Analytics Tracking]
    G --> H[Google Analytics]
    F --> I[Data Retrieval]
    I --> J[Component Rendering]
    J --> K[UI Update]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- [App.tsx](App.tsx)
- [index.tsx](index.tsx)
- [index.html](index.html)

### Core Modules
- [App.tsx](App.tsx) - Main application component with routing and state management
- [Header.tsx](components/Header.tsx) - Navigation and theme toggle
- [HeroSection.tsx](components/HeroSection.tsx) - Main landing page content
- [ServicesSection.tsx](components/ServicesSection.tsx) - Service offerings display
- [ContactSection.tsx](components/ContactSection.tsx) - AI strategy generation form
- [Footer.tsx](components/Footer.tsx) - Site footer with navigation and newsletter

### Configuration Approach
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts) - Vite build configuration
- [package.json](package.json) - Project dependencies and scripts
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json) - TypeScript configuration
- [vercel.json](vercel.json) - Vercel deployment configuration
- [constants.tsx](constants.tsx) - Application constants and configuration data

### External Dependencies
- [package.json](package.json) - Lists all dependencies including React, Supabase, and Google GenAI

### Integration Points
- [services/supabase.ts](services/supabase.ts) - Supabase database integration
- [services/analytics.ts](services/analytics.ts) - Google Analytics integration
- [api/brave.js](api/brave.js) - API integration (specific purpose not clear from filename)
- [index.html](index.html) - Calendly widget and Google tag integration

### Component Relationships
```mermaid
graph LR
    App[App] --> Header[Header]
    App --> Footer[Footer]
    App --> ChatWidget[ChatWidget]
    App --> CookieConsent[CookieConsentBanner]
    App --> CalendlyModal[CalendlyModal]
    App --> PasswordModal[PasswordModal]
    App --> BlogAdminDashboard[BlogAdminDashboard]
    App --> MainContent[MainContent]
    MainContent --> HeroSection[HeroSection]
    MainContent --> ServicesSection[ServicesSection]
    MainContent --> HowItWorksSection[HowItWorksSection]
    MainContent --> ContactSection[ContactSection]
    MainContent --> PricingSection[PricingSection]
    MainContent --> AILaunchpadSection[AILaunchpadSection]
    MainContent --> ResourceCenterSection[ResourceCenterSection]
    MainContent --> TestimonialsSection[TestimonialsSection]
    MainContent --> FAQSection[FAQSection]
    MainContent --> LetsTalkSection[LetsTalkSection]
    HeroSection --> InteractiveNetwork[InteractiveNetwork]
    HeroSection --> PartnerLogo[PartnerLogo]
    ContactSection --> MarkdownRenderer[MarkdownRenderer]
    ContactSection --> DynamicLoader[DynamicLoader]
    ContactSection --> StyledText[StyledText]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The Synaptix Studio website offers a comprehensive suite of features designed to showcase their AI automation services and generate leads:

1. **Interactive AI Strategy Generator**: Users can input their business details and receive a custom AI-generated strategy
2. **Free AI Business Tools**: A collection of AI-powered tools for ad copy generation, subject line testing, and more
3. **Dynamic Content Management**: Admin interface for managing blog posts and content
4. **Lead Generation Forms**: Multiple forms for newsletter signup, contact, and strategy consultation
5. **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with adaptive layouts for all device sizes
6. **Dark/Light Theme Toggle**: User preference for visual theme
7. **Analytics Integration**: Comprehensive tracking of user interactions and conversions

### Implementation Highlights
- [App.tsx](App.tsx#L1-L595) - Implements hash-based routing and theme management
- [ContactSection.tsx](components/ContactSection.tsx#L1-L400) - AI strategy generation with Google GenAI integration
- [HeroSection.tsx](components/HeroSection.tsx#L1-L436) - Dynamic content with animated taglines and service cards
- [services/supabase.ts](services/supabase.ts#L1-L278) - Comprehensive Supabase integration for data persistence
- [services/analytics.ts](services/analytics.ts#L1-L40) - Google Analytics event tracking implementation
- [constants.tsx](constants.tsx#L1-L799) - Centralized configuration and content management

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Home
    Home --> Services: Click Services link
    Home --> Blog: Click Blog link
    Home --> AITools: Click AI Tools link
    Home --> Contact: Click Contact link
    Home --> AI_Strategy: Submit business details
    AI_Strategy --> Generating: Processing with AI
    Generating --> Strategy_Results: AI generates strategy
    Strategy_Results --> Download: User downloads PDF
    Strategy_Results --> Copy: User copies to clipboard
    Strategy_Results --> Book_Demo: User books demo
    Book_Demo --> Calendly: Opens Calendly modal
    Calendly --> Confirmation: Meeting scheduled
    Home --> Admin: Type 'admin' sequence
    Admin --> Password: Enter password
    Password --> Dashboard: Access blog admin
    Dashboard --> Edit_Posts: Manage blog content
    Edit_Posts --> Save: Save changes to database
```
</blog>
